title: lichen_l2_recv does nothing but return NET_CONTINUE
description:
lichen_l2.c:106-113 implements L2 recv handler that just returns NET_CONTINUE with a comment explaining the packet is already decompressed.

What goes wrong as-is: A function that does nothing except return a constant is the definition of a no-op. The comment explains why, but the reader might wonder if this could be NULL in the NET_L2_INIT registration.

Note: Zephyr's net_l2 API may require a non-NULL callback. If so, this is unavoidable boilerplate. If not, passing NULL would be clearer. Verify NET_L2_INIT requirements.
close_reason: By design: Zephyr net_l2 API requires non-NULL recv callback
